content_markdown: The albedo for the oceans is lower than that for glaciers. This is because, compared to ice, sea water

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: B
options.text: has a greater specific heat capacity.

options.label: C
options.text: has a greater coefficient of volume expansion.

options.label: D
options.text: absorbs a greater amount of radiative power.

vibe_explanation: Albedo is defined as the ratio of reflected radiation to incident radiation. A lower albedo means that less radiation is reflected and, consequently, more radiation is absorbed. Therefore, sea water (with a lower albedo) absorbs more radiative power than ice/glaciers.
